554185195.name: Clewiston Museum & Chamber of Commerce
554185195.note: Discrepancy: prior record guessed this footprint was the Dixie Crystal Theatre based on commons photos. All 9 usable street-level frames (823390702411869.jpg, 957659778527959.jpg, 788366492269470.jpg, 803482644039800.jpg, 167019682598187.jpg, 612117050706332.jpg, 627163309193121.jpg, 2071744059678824.jpg, 1236589494749326.jpg) consistently show a low, single-story, flat-roofed cream/beige commercial building with arched garage-door-style openings, currently fronted by a Jeep/truck dealership lot, labeled 'CLEWISTON MUSEUM & CHAMBER OF COMMERCE' in signage visible on the facade. No art-deco theatre with a stepped parapet or marquee appears in any frame. Rewritten to match what the imagery actually shows; confidence kept at needs-confirmation since this may be a footprint/address mismatch rather than a true theatre sighting, and Richard should confirm whether the theatre building exists elsewhere nearby.

Remaining daytime frames (799081904635367.jpg, 687018830366561.jpg and others) only show the distant dealership-lot road scene shared with building -1002452's Clewiston Museum facade, not a distinct facade for this footprint. Could not confidently describe from available imagery.
